_notes: [FALSIFIED 2026-05-27: seed31 run hit abort criterion R5=0.70 < 0.75 (full trajectory R0=0.61, R3=0.66, R4=0.70, R5=0.70, R6=0.68, R7=0.70, R8=0.69 — flat plateau matches v3 baseline at 0.69-0.71, ZERO LIFT from the budget knobs). The step-budget-bound hypothesis is ruled out; the AlfWorld ceiling is NOT truncation-bound on this slice. Next-hypothesis pivot: see goalcond config (TurnRDV2_alfworld_SOTA_10round_mlpr32_v3_goalcond.json) for the supervision-mismatch alternative. KEPT for negative-evidence reproducibility; do NOT relaunch.] xlbudget variant of TurnRDV2_alfworld_SOTA_10round_mlpr32_v3 (plan `alfworld_xlbudget_break_ceiling`). HYPOTHESIS: the v3 R9=0.71 / milestone R12=0.73 plateau on the biased easy slice is STRUCTURAL (step-budget-bound), not credit-assignment-bound. EVIDENCE: re-analysis of `reports/turnrd_credit_assignment_demo/v3_R9_probe.jsonl` (50 eval episodes, R9): (a) 11/11 failures truncate at n_turns == 40 (the probe's looser cap; the actual RL eval cap is 30, so even more would truncate there), (b) 6/39 successes already need > 30 turns (16, 16, 19, 20, 25, 37) — the 30-cap is actively suppressing winnable episodes, (c) 8/11 failures show forward-progress patterns in their last 10 turns (systematic drawer search, mid-task progression), (d) 3/11 failures are policy-dead-zones (IR=0 in last 10 turns, repeated identical actions) — these are a HARD FLOOR the budget lift cannot move and need credit-assignment or exploration work. CHANGES vs v3: (a) env.max_steps 40 -> 60 (TextWorld adapter step cap headroom matching the new turn cap), (b) NEW env.max_turns=50 (overrides app_train_loop's default 30 via the new JSON plumbing; 50 gives the forward-progress failures the ~10-20 extra turns they need to finish). DELIBERATELY NOT CHANGED: train/eval max_action_tokens stays at 48. The probe's evidence is decisive: max action length across all 11 failure trajectories is 6 words (~10 tokens); zero actions exceed 8 words. Lifting the per-action token cap to 96 would be pure no-op and confound the experiment. Replay/ckpt paths re-prefixed to ...xlbudget/ so the existing v3 replay buffer + per-round adapters are preserved for old-vs-new comparison. EVERYTHING ELSE identical to v3: rank-32 MLP+attn LoRA, alpha=0.5, v_projection=True, recency_decay_half_life=4.0, num_train_games=400, lambda_value=1.0, TURNRD_EPOCHS=5 (launcher default), same SFT warm-start adapter. REALISTIC GOAL (probe-derived): push eval.pct_success from 0.71-0.73 to 0.78-0.85. The probe at 40 turns already achieves 0.78, and saving 5-8 of the 11 progress-bound failures at 50 turns puts the upper-end estimate at ~0.86. The 3 dead-zone failures (~6% of eval) cap the achievable ceiling at ~0.94 even with infinite turns. ABORT CRITERIA: R0 < 0.62 (budget lift HURT R0), or R5 < 0.75 (ceiling wasn't truncation-bound after all). NEW OBSERVABILITY: eval_block now also emits `empty_outputs` and `empty_outputs_per_episode_avg` (was tracked by collectors.py:228 but never persisted). If empty_outputs_per_episode_avg > 1 at R9, the next-tuning signal is stop-token / temperature hardening rather than further budget lift.
